message:
fix(policy): emergency escalation to Enforce, grace period, pressure-adaptive scans

Major policy engine evolution for more aggressive and stable emergency response:

Emergency escalation changes:
- Emergency now escalates directly to Enforce mode (was Canary) for faster
  disk pressure relief — Canary was too conservative under real pressure
- Add EMERGENCY_GRACE_PERIOD_SECS (30 min) to prevent escalate→breach→fallback
  deadlock cycle: if we just escalated, don't immediately enter fallback on
  the next breach detection
- Reduce FALLBACK_EMERGENCY_ESCALATION_SECS from 15 min to 5 min for faster
  emergency response
- Track emergency_escalated_at timestamp for grace period enforcement
- Restore canary budget exhaustion → FallbackSafe transition (was removed
  in previous iteration, needed for safety)

Scanner tuning:
- Increase SCAN_ENTRY_BUDGET from 100K to 500K entries for thorough scans
- Increase SCAN_TIME_BUDGET_SECS from 60s to 300s baseline
- Add pressure-adaptive scan budgets: 3x multiplier for Red/Critical zones,
  2x for Orange — critical pressure needs deeper scans to find deletables
- Add /data/tmp to high-score temporary path patterns in scoring
- Add /home and /root to default scan paths in config
- Lower min_score threshold 0.45→0.35 for broader candidate discovery
- Lower calibration_floor 0.55→0.40 for more aggressive calibration

Policy tuning:
- recovery_clean_windows: tune to 3 (faster recovery detection)
- calibration_breach_windows: tune to 8 (balanced breach sensitivity)
- Fix emergency escalation log message: "canary" → "enforce"

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
